'''Martin Hall''' (born 5 December 1968) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer and coach. During his playing career, Hall played {{rlp|hk}} at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Hull FC]].

As a coach, he had stints with [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. He was the head coach of [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] between 2006 and 2010.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="swecho" /><ref name="faxco">
{{cite news
|url=http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/local/Coach-adds-four-to-squad.3202149.jp
|title=Coach adds four to squad
|last=Roberts|first=James
|date=14 September 2007
|work=Halifax Courier
|accessdate=27 February 2010}}
</ref> He was also the head coach of the [[Wales national rugby league team]] for three years between 2005 and 2007.<ref name="swecho">
{{Cite news
|url= http://www.walesonline.co.uk/sports/rugby-league-news/2007/05/25/r-league-crusaders-out-to-derail-halifax-91466-19191277/
|title=Crusaders out to derail Halifax
|work=South Wales Echo
|accessdate=27 February 2010
|date=25 May 2007
}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Rochdale Hornets===
Hall started his career with [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] before joining [[Rochdale Hornets]] in 1989, with the transfer fee of £10,000 being set by a tribunal.<ref>{{cite news |title=Tribunal boost for Hornets |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/BL/0000310/19890812/071/0071 |work=Rochdale Observer |date=12 August 1989 |page=71 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

Martin Hall played in [[Rochdale Hornets]] 14–24 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1991 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wilderspool Stadium]], [[Warrington]], on Sunday 20 October 1991.

===Wigan===
Hall was signed by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in January 1993 for a fee of £35,000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Hornets sell off Hall to Wigan |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-hornets-sell-off-hall-to-wigan-1478141.html |website=The Independent |access-date=2 January 2024 |date=12 January 1993}}</ref> After the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]], Hall travelled with defending champions [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] to Brisbane, playing from the interchange bench in their [[1994 World Club Challenge]] victory over Australian premiers, the [[Brisbane Broncos]].

Hall played {{rlp|hk}} in [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s 40–10 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1994–95 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95 season]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 28 January 1995.<ref name="28 January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|title=28th January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=14 July 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714151036/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|url-status=dead}}</ref> He played {{rlp|hk}}, and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s 30–10 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[1995 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 29 April 1995.<ref name="indy">{{cite news |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/farrells-accuracy-fells-halifax-1599917.html |title=Farrell's accuracy fells Halifax |last=Hadfield |first=Dave |date=7 September 1995 |work=[[The Independent]] |accessdate=27 February 2010}}</ref>

Hall played {{rlp|hk}} in the 25–17 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96 season]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13 January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|archive-date=7 October 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
Hall was appointed as coach at [[Rochdale Hornets]] in November 2000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Peacock strut to England's liking |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/peacock-strut-to-england-s-liking-624051.html |website=The Independent |access-date=2 January 2024 |date=9 November 2000}}</ref> He was named National League One Coach of the Year in 2003, but resigned at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rochdale in turmoil after Hall resigns |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/rochdale-in-turmoil-after-hall-resigns-80046.html |website=The Independent |access-date=2 January 2024 |date=26 November 2003}}</ref>

Hall took over the head coaching job at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in June 2006, replacing [[Anthony Farrell]].<ref name="bbc1">
{{cite news
|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/5083962.stm
|title=Hall replaces Farrell at Halifax|date=15 June 2006
|work=Sport online
|publisher=[[BBC]]
|accessdate=27 February 2010}}
</ref>
